findTilePosition.name: Find Tile Position
findTilePosition.description: Write a function that finds the position of a specific letter tile in the rack. Return "Move to position X" if found, or "Error: Tile not on rack" if not.

tileAtStart.name: Tile at start
tileAtStart.description: The letter is the first tile in the rack.

tileInMiddle.name: Tile in middle
tileInMiddle.description: The letter is in the middle of the rack.

tileAtEnd.name: Tile at end
tileAtEnd.description: The letter is the last tile in the rack.

tileNotFound.name: Tile not found
tileNotFound.description: The letter isn't in the rack.

emptyRack.name: Empty rack
emptyRack.description: An empty rack has no tiles to find.

firstOfDuplicates.name: First of duplicates
firstOfDuplicates.description: When the letter appears multiple times, return the first position.

longerRack.name: Longer rack
longerRack.description: Finding a tile in a longer rack.

trackPosition.question: How do I keep track of where I am in the rack?
trackPosition.answer: Use a position variable starting at 1. Each time you look at a new tile, you'll increase it by 1.

lookAtEachTile.question: How do I look at each tile?
lookAtEachTile.answer: A for-each loop gives you each character of the rack in turn.

onFound.question: What do I do when I find the letter?
onFound.answer: Build the success message. Convert the position number to a string, then combine it with the surrounding text the exercise asks for using concatenation (`+`) or a template string. Return immediately. There's no need to keep looking.

positionAlwaysWrong.question: Why is my position always wrong?
positionAlwaysWrong.answer: Make sure you're increasing the position counter every iteration, not just sometimes. Otherwise every tile reports the same position.

notInRack.question: What if the letter isn't in the rack?
notInRack.answer: Only after the loop has finished without finding the letter should you return the error message. Returning the error inside the loop would give up after the first non-matching tile.

numberToString.description: Convert a number to its string representation (provided by level stdlib)
